claimFamily.name: Index Deletion And Forced Selling
claimFamily.parentClaimText: SENEA was deleted from the S&P SmallCap 600 in July 2023, producing forced selling pressure independent of fundamentals.

verdictRationale: The local source register documents source S-002 only as a retrieval pointer to the S&P Dow Jones Indices index-action archive; no announcement-date, effective-date, ETF-holdings, price/volume, bid-ask, or factor-return data has been collected into the research artifact tree. The claims-source-pack scope field explicitly defers primary-source retrieval and pass/fail verification to downstream tasks (financial-rebuild, industry-attack, attribution), and open question Q-003 in the same pack remains unanswered. Per the acceptance rule that an absent reliable price/volume or ETF ownership dataset must be labeled `unresolved` with the exact data needed enumerated, every sub-claim below is `unresolved` and the family verdict is `unresolved` with high confidence in the verdict itself.

claimsTested.text: SENEA was deleted from the S&P SmallCap 600 in July 2023.
claimsTested.reason: Source S-002 is a pointer to the S&P Dow Jones Indices index-action archive that the source-pack scope marks as not yet retrieved; the precise announcement date and effective date are still tracked as open question Q-003.
requiredEvidence: S&P Dow Jones Indices index-action press release naming SENEA, with announcement date, effective date, and stated reason.
Reconciliation of the announcement to a corresponding constituent-change file (typical SP DJI rebalance file naming).

claimsTested.text: The deletion mechanically forced index-tracking ETFs to sell SENEA, producing concentrated, non-fundamental sell flow at the effective date.
claimsTested.reason: No ETF-holdings or fund-flow source is on the local register. Expected mechanical sell volume cannot be computed without the IJR / SLY / VIOO (and any other S&P SmallCap 600 tracker) holdings as of the day before the effective date.
requiredEvidence: iShares Core S&P Small-Cap ETF (IJR) holdings file as of T-2 - 1.
SPDR S&P 600 Small Cap ETF (SLY) holdings file as of T-2 - 1.
Vanguard S&P Small-Cap 600 ETF (VIOO) holdings file as of T-2 - 1.
Any other S&P SmallCap 600 tracker holdings on T-2 - 1 to capture the full mechanical bid.
Cross-check vs SENEA Form 10-Q / 10-K share-count and Form 13F holders for total institutional context.
missingSources: ETF holdings vendor (e.g. issuer fund-page CSVs, or a vendor like FactSet/Bloomberg)

claimsTested.text: Post-deletion liquidity (average daily volume, bid-ask spread, Amihud illiquidity) materially deteriorated versus a pre-deletion baseline.
claimsTested.reason: No price, volume, or quote series has been ingested into the research tree (research/senea/ contains only claims-source-pack.json, claims-source-pack.md, and source-register.csv). Without OHLCV plus quote data, the pre/post liquidity comparison cannot be computed.
requiredEvidence: SENEA daily OHLCV for the window [T-1 - 250, T-2 + 250] business days.
Daily best-bid/best-offer or TAQ-derived spreads for the same window.
Pre/post averages for ADV, bid-ask spread, and Amihud illiquidity, with statistical significance tests.
missingSources: Market-data vendor (exchange feed or vendor such as Refinitiv, Bloomberg, Polygon)

claimsTested.text: Cumulative abnormal returns inside the announcement-to-effective-date window are negative and statistically distinguishable from market, small-cap, and food-sector returns, in the direction predicted by the index-deletion literature.
claimsTested.reason: The plan's nextArtifact for C-007 is artifacts/event_study.csv (sp600_deletion_window); that file has not been written. No estimation-window factor-return or sector-return data is on the register.
requiredEvidence: Market-model and Fama-French-5 + momentum regressions over [T-1 - 250, T-1 - 30].
Cumulative abnormal returns over [T-1 - 1, T-1 + 1], [T-1, T-2], [T-2, T-2 + 5], and [T-2 + 6, T-2 + 60].
Sector control using a packaged-foods peer basket or a packaged-food ETF (e.g. PBJ, KXI components mapped to U.S. food).
p-values or bootstrap intervals on each CAR window.
missingSources: Factor-return source (e.g. Ken French data library)

claimsTested.text: Observed event-window weakness, and any persistent post-event drift, cannot be explained by Seneca fundamental disclosures, small-cap value factor moves, or food-sector moves.
claimsTested.reason: No catalog of contemporaneous Seneca SEC filings or press releases in the [T-1 - 30, T-2 + 30] window has been produced; no factor or sector returns are loaded; the plan's Decision Gate 4 requires factor/sector controls before attribution can pass.
requiredEvidence: Tagged catalog of every SENEA 10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, press release, and dividend/buyback announcement inside [T-1 - 30, T-2 + 30] (sources S-001, S-007).
Russell 2000 / Russell 2000 Value reconstitution dates inside the window so that S&P-driven flows can be separated from Russell-driven flows.
Small-cap value factor (HML, SMB, R2000V) cumulative returns across the same windows.

sourcesUsed.title: Seneca Foods Corporation FY2025 Annual Report on Form 10-K (LIFO accounting, $359.3m LIFO reserve, FIFO-adjusted earnings, FIFO EBITDA disclosures)
sourcesUsed.publisher: Seneca Foods Corporation / U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission

sourcesUsed.title: S&P Dow Jones Indices announcement of the July 2023 deletion of Seneca Foods (SENEA) from the S&P SmallCap 600
Seneca Foods historical 10-K and 10-Q filings (FY2005-FY2025) for balance-sheet and inventory reconstruction
sourcesUsed.publisher: Seneca Foods Corporation / U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission

missingSources.description: Daily OHLCV plus best-bid/best-offer for SENEA covering [T-1 - 250, T-2 + 250] business days.
Holdings files for IJR, SLY, VIOO and any other S&P SmallCap 600 trackers as of T-2 - 1, plus subsequent rebalances.
Fama-French-5 + momentum factor returns and Russell 2000 / Russell 2000 Value daily returns covering the estimation and event windows.
Russell 2000 / Russell 2000 Value reconstitution calendar for 2023 and SENEA membership before/after.
Packaged-foods sector benchmark or peer basket return series for the event window.

evidence.description: S&P Dow Jones Indices announcement of SENEA deletion from the S&P SmallCap 600.
Effective date of SENEA deletion (close of business on rebalance day).
Aggregate SENEA share count held by S&P SmallCap 600 trackers (IJR, SLY, VIOO and other) as of T-2 - 1.
Observed SENEA volume on T-2 vs 60-day pre-event median ADV (one-day rebalance spike test).
Pre vs post-event ADV, bid-ask spread, and Amihud illiquidity ratio.
Cumulative abnormal returns from market-model and Fama-French-5 + momentum regressions across multiple event windows.
Catalog of every SENEA SEC filing and press release inside [T-1 - 30, T-2 + 30], tagged fundamental vs non-fundamental.
Cumulative HML, SMB, R2000V, and packaged-foods returns over the same event windows used for IS-4.
Russell 2000 / Russell 2000 Value membership and reconstitution dates inside [T-1 - 30, T-2 + 30].

contraryEvidence.description: Concurrent fundamentals (Seneca FY2024 reporting cycle) may explain part of the cumulative move; CARs can be biased without a fundamentals filter.
Mid-2023 small-cap value factor weakness can absorb part of the negative drift; without an HML / R2000V control the index-deletion signal is overstated.
Russell reconstitution typically lands in late June; if SENEA's Russell membership changed in the same window, some forced-selling volume belongs to Russell flows rather than S&P SmallCap 600 flows.

S&P SmallCap 600 deletions are pre-announced; arbitrageurs frequently front-run, so cumulative abnormal returns over [T-1, T-2] can be near zero by the effective date even when the flow story is real.
Post-deletion ADV decline can reflect general small-cap volume contraction in 2023-2024 rather than SENEA-specific liquidity loss; a peer-control comparison is required.

openQuestions.question: What is the precise announcement date, effective date, and S&P-cited reason for SENEA's deletion from the S&P SmallCap 600 in July 2023?
What were SENEA share counts and dollar exposure inside IJR, SLY, VIOO, and any other S&P SmallCap 600 trackers as of the close on T-2 - 1, and how do they sum to expected mechanical sell volume?
What are the pre/post averages for SENEA average daily volume, bid-ask spread, and Amihud illiquidity, and are the differences statistically significant?
What are the cumulative abnormal returns over [T-1 - 1, T-1 + 1], [T-1, T-2], [T-2, T-2 + 5], and [T-2 + 6, T-2 + 60] under both market-model and Fama-French-5 + momentum specifications?
Which Seneca filings and press releases fall inside [T-1 - 30, T-2 + 30], and how do CARs change when fundamental-news days are excluded?
Did SENEA's Russell 2000 / Russell 2000 Value membership change inside the same window, and how much of any forced-selling volume can be attributed to Russell rather than S&P flows?
